title: [FIRRTL][InferWidths] Back-prop for mux selector operand; different than subaccess
body:
Before getting into details:

The FIRRTL spec does say the selector will be inferred to width `1`, so this may be "working as intended" / spec detail to be resolved.  It is inconsistent with subaccess, as noted below.

Onwards to the issue, consider:

```firrtl
circuit Mux:
  module Mux:
    input x : UInt<0>
    output y : UInt
    output z : UInt

    wire w : UInt
    w <= x

    y <= w

    wire buffer : UInt
    buffer <= w
    z <= mux(buffer, UInt(1), UInt(2))
```
vs

```firrtl
circuit Mux:
  module Mux:
    input x : UInt<0>
    output y : UInt
    output z : UInt

    wire w : UInt
    w <= x

    y <= w

    ; wire buffer : UInt
    ; buffer <= w
    z <= mux(w, UInt(1), UInt(2))
```
 
Which produces the following outputs (1.34.0 to avoid #5391):
```systemverilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.34.0
module Mux(
  output [1:0] z
);

  assign z = 2'h2;
endmodule
```
and
```systemverilog
module Mux(
  output       y,
  output [1:0] z
);

  assign y = 1'h0;
  assign z = 2'h2;
endmodule
```

Note that the presence of the wire `buffer` (which could be optimized/elided for various reasons) changes the widths and presence of hardware on different path.

Probably we don't want any back-propagation at all (other than `attach`), or should rework how widths are inferred in spec (perhaps to something more unification-oriented + solving together, vs minimum to make input connections legal / forward-only).

----

This behavior does not happen for the "equivalent" subaccess operation, which is a bit inconsistent (thanks to @seldridge/@uenoku for pointing this out).
